description:
From the side viewI believe this bird to be a non-breeding male or possible a November juvenile. A chunky compact bunting, shorter tail, with a seed bill, gray upper and silver lower mandible. Larger in weight and size than the mixed flock of White-crowned and House Sparrows. The secondary feathers, primary and greater coverts were black, edged with rich tawny showing a bright wing bar look. The folded primaries were black. The crown and mantle were muted brown and tawny modeling. The flanks were white changing to tawny with muted black spots. The head had a plain brown crown and auricular patch with white surrounding the lower edge extending into the submoustachial area. It
had black surrounding and bill with a white supraloral stripe and tawny thin
eye ring. Muted tawny supercilium behind eye to nape.From the front viewHeavy black malar stripe and surrounding bill including chin.
White with smudgy black stripes thinning to belly. Obvious white submoustachial
and supraloral stripes.
photographer: Photographer: Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe
